steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says average ratings can be misleading when few users have rated a game, but this game has over 8,000 raters. The blank describes what kind of number 8,000 is — specifically, large enough to make the average trustworthy. The word must mean "sufficient" or "enough."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — excessive: excessive means too much — more than needed. The passage doesn't suggest 8,000 is an unreasonably large number, just that it's enough. ✗
  • Choice B — adequate: adequate means sufficient — exactly enough to serve the purpose. 8,000 users is an adequate number to feel confident the rating is reliable. ✓
  • Choice C — accurate: accurate describes precision or correctness, not quantity. 8,000 is a number of raters, not a measure of accuracy. ✗
  • Choice D — insufficient: insufficient means not enough — the opposite of what the passage argues. The passage says 8,000 makes the rating reliable. ✗
That is the correct answer.
